volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Antigone (Section 756 - Section 931)

sections.label: Section 756
sections.original: γυναικὸς ὢν δούλευμα μὴ κώτιλλέ με .
sections.translation: You woman ’ s slave , do not try to cajole me .

sections.label: Section 757
sections.original: βούλει λέγειν τι καὶ λέγων μηδὲν κλύειν ;
sections.translation: Do you want to have your say and then have done without a reply ?

sections.label: Section 758
sections.original: ἄληθες ; ἀλλʼ οὐ τόνδʼ Ὄλυμπον , ἴσθʼ ὅτι ,
sections.translation: Is that so ? By Olympus above — know this well — you will have no joy for taunting me over and above your censures .

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: ἄγαγε τὸ μῖσος ὡς κατʼ ὄμματʼ αὐτίκα
sections.translation: Bring out that hated thing , so that with him looking on she may die right now in her bridegroom ’ s presence and at his side !

sections.label: Section 762
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ ἔμοιγε , τοῦτο μὴ δόξῃς ποτέ ,
sections.translation: No , not at my side will she die — do not ever imagine it . Nor shall you ever look at me and set eyes on my face again .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: ὡς τοῖς θέλουσι τῶν φίλων μαίνῃ συνών .
sections.translation: Indulge in your madness now with whomever of your friends can endure it . Exit Haemon .

sections.label: Section 766
sections.original: ἁνήρ , ἄναξ , βέβηκεν ἐξ ὀργῆς ταχύς ·
sections.translation: The man is gone , King Creon , in anger and haste . A young mind is fierce when stung .

sections.label: Section 768
sections.original: δράτω · φρονείτω μεῖζον ἢ κατʼ ἄνδρʼ ἰών ·
sections.translation: Let him do — no !— let him plan something more immense than befits a man . Farewell to him ! Still he will not save these two girls from death .

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: ἄμφω γὰρ αὐτὼ καὶ κατακτεῖναι νοεῖς ;
sections.translation: Then the pair of them , you really intend to kill them both ?

sections.label: Section 771
sections.original: οὐ τήν γε μὴ θιγοῦσαν · εὖ γὰρ οὖν λέγεις .
sections.translation: Not the one who did not put her hands to the burial . You are right .

sections.label: Section 772
sections.original: μόρῳ δὲ ποίῳ καί σφε βουλεύει κτανεῖν ;
sections.translation: And by what mode of death do you mean to kill the other ?

sections.label: Section 773
sections.original: ἄγων ἔρημος ἔνθʼ ἂν ᾖ βροτῶν στίβος
sections.translation: I will take her where the path is deserted , unvisited by men , and entomb her alive in a rocky vault ,

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: φορβῆς τοσοῦτον ὡς ἄγος μόνον προθείς ,
sections.translation: setting out a ration of food , but only as much as piety requires so that all the city may escape defilement . And praying there to Hades , the only god she worships , perhaps she will obtain immunity from death , or else will learn , at last , even this late ,

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: πόνος περισσός ἐστι τἀν Ἅιδου σέβειν .
sections.translation: that it is fruitless labor to revere the dead . Exit Creon .

sections.label: Section 1.781
sections.original: Ἔρως ἀνίκατε μάχαν , Ἔρως , ὃς ἐν κτήμασι πίπτεις ,
sections.translation: Love , the unconquered in battle , Love , you who descend upon riches , and watch the night through on a girl ’ s soft cheek ,

sections.label: Section 1.785
sections.original: φοιτᾷς δʼ ὑπερπόντιος ἔν τʼ ἀγρονόμοις αὐλαῖς ·
sections.translation: you roam over the sea and among the homes of men in the wilds . Neither can any immortal escape you ,

sections.label: Section 1.790
sections.original: οὔθʼ ἁμερίων σέ γʼ ἀνθρώπων . ὁ δʼ ἔχων μέμηνεν .
sections.translation: nor any man whose life lasts for a day . He who has known you is driven to madness .

sections.label: Section 1.791
sections.original: σὺ καὶ δικαίων ἀδίκους φρένας παρασπᾷς ἐπὶ λώβᾳ ,
sections.translation: You seize the minds of just men and drag them to injustice , to their ruin . You it is who have incited this conflict of men whose flesh and blood are one .

sections.label: Section 1.795
sections.original: νικᾷ δʼ ἐναργὴς βλεφάρων ἵμερος εὐλέκτρου
sections.translation: But victory belongs to radiant Desire swelling from the eyes of the sweet-bedded bride . Desire sits enthroned in power beside the mighty laws .

sections.label: Section 1.800
sections.original: θεσμῶν . ἄμαχος γὰρ ἐμπαίζει θεὸς Ἀφροδίτα .
sections.translation: For in all this divine Aphrodite plays her irresistible game .

sections.label: Section 801
sections.original: νῦν δʼ ἤδη ʼγὼ καὐτὸς θεσμῶν
sections.translation: But now , witnessing this , I too am carried beyond the bounds of loyalty . The power fails me to keep back my streaming tears any longer , when I see Antigone making her way to the chamber where all are laid to rest ,

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: τήνδʼ Ἀντιγόνην ἀνύτουσαν .
sections.translation: now her bridal chamber .

sections.label: Section 1.806
sections.original: ὁρᾶτʼ ἔμʼ , ὦ γᾶς πατρίας πολῖται , τὰν νεάταν ὁδὸν
sections.translation: Citizens of my fatherland , see me setting out on my last journey , looking at my last sunlight ,

sections.label: Section 1.810
sections.original: κοὔποτʼ αὖθις . ἀλλά μʼ ὁ παγκοίτας Ἅιδας ζῶσαν ἄγει
sections.translation: and never again . No , Hades who lays all to rest leads me living to Acheron ’ s shore , though I have not had my due portion of the chant that brings the bride , nor has any hymn been mine

sections.label: Section 1.815
sections.original: πώ μέ τις ὕμνος ὕμνησεν , ἀλλʼ Ἀχέροντι νυμφεύσω .
sections.translation: for the crowning of marriage . Instead the lord of Acheron will be my groom .

sections.label: Section 817
sections.original: οὐκοῦν κλεινὴ καὶ ἔπαινον ἔχουσʼ
sections.translation: Then in glory and with praise you depart to that deep place of the dead , neither struck by wasting sickness ,

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: οὔτε ξιφέων ἐπίχειρα λαχοῦσʼ ,
sections.translation: nor having won the wages of the sword . No , guided by your own laws and still alive , unlike any mortal before , you will descend to Hades .

sections.label: Section 1.823
sections.original: ἤκουσα δὴ λυγροτάταν ὀλέσθαι τὰν Φρυγίαν ξέναν
sections.translation: I have heard with my own ears how our Phrygian guest , the daughter of Tantalus , perished

sections.label: Section 1.825
sections.original: Ταντάλου Σιπύλῳ πρὸς ἄκρῳ , τὰν κισσὸς ὡς ἀτενὴς
sections.translation: in so much suffering on steep Sipylus — how , like clinging ivy , the sprouting stone subdued her . And the rains , as men tell , do not leave her melting form , nor does the snow ,

sections.label: Section 1.830
sections.original: χιών τʼ οὐδαμὰ λείπει , τέγγει δʼ ὑπʼ ὀφρύσι παγκλαύτοις
sections.translation: but beneath her weeping lids she dampens her collar . Most like hers is the god-sent fate that leads me to my rest .

sections.label: Section 834
sections.original: ἀλλὰ θεός τοι καὶ θεογεννής ,
sections.translation: Yet she was a goddess , as you know , and the offspring of gods ,

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δὲ βροτοὶ καὶ θνητογενεῖς .
sections.translation: while we are mortals and mortal-born . Still it is a great thing for a woman who has died to have it said of her that she shared the lot of the godlike in her life , and afterwards , in death .

sections.label: Section 2.839
sections.original: οἴμοι γελῶμαι . τί με , πρὸς θεῶν πατρῴων .
sections.translation: Ah , you mock me ! In the name of our fathers ’ gods ,

sections.label: Section 2.840
sections.original: οὐκ οἰχομέναν ὑβρίζεις , ἀλλʼ ἐπίφαντον ;
sections.translation: why do you not wait to abuse me until after I have gone , and not to my face , O my city , and you , her wealthy citizens ? Ah , spring of Dirce , and you holy ground of Thebes whose chariots are many ,

sections.label: Section 2.845
sections.original: Θήβας τʼ εὐαρμάτου ἄλσος , ἔμπας ξυμμάρτυρας ὔμμʼ ἐπικτῶμαι ,
sections.translation: you , at least , will bear me witness how unwept by loved ones , and by what laws I go to the rock-closed prison of my unheard-of tomb ! Ah , misery !

sections.label: Section 2.850
sections.original: ἰὼ δύστανος , βροτοῖς οὔτε νεκροῖς κυροῦσα
sections.translation: I have no home among men or with the shades , no home with the living or with the dead .

sections.label: Section 3.853
sections.original: προβᾶσʼ ἐπʼ ἔσχατον θράσους
sections.translation: You have rushed headlong to the far limits of daring , and against the high throne of Justice

sections.label: Section 3.855
sections.original: προσέπεσες , ὦ τέκνον , πολύ ·
sections.translation: you have fallen , my daughter , fallen heavily . But in this ordeal you are paying for some paternal crime .

sections.label: Section 2.858
sections.original: ἔψαυσας ἀλγεινοτάτας ἐμοὶ μερίμνας ,
sections.translation: You have touched on my most bitter thought

sections.label: Section 2.860
sections.original: ἁμετέρου πότμου κλεινοῖς Λαβδακίδαισιν .
sections.translation: and moved my ever-renewed pity for my father and for the entire doom ordained for us , the famed house of Labdacus . Oh , the horrors of our mother ’ s bed ! Oh , the slumbers of the wretched mother at the side

sections.label: Section 2.865
sections.original: ἆται κοιμήματά τʼ αὐτογέννητʼ ἐμῷ πατρὶ δυσμόρου ματρός ,
sections.translation: of her own son , my own father ! What manner of parents gave me my miserable being ! It is to them that I go like this , accursed and unwed , to share their home .

sections.label: Section 2.870
sections.original: ἰὼ δυσπότμων κασίγνητε γάμων κυρήσας ,
sections.translation: Ah , my brother , the marriage you made was doomed , and by dying you killed me still alive !

sections.label: Section 3.872
sections.original: σέβειν μὲν εὐσέβειά τις ,
sections.translation: Your pious action shows a certain reverence , but an offence against power can no way be tolerated by him who has power in his keeping .

sections.label: Section 3.875
sections.original: σὲ δʼ αὐτόγνωτος ὤλεσʼ ὀργά .
sections.translation: Your self-willed disposition is what has destroyed you .

sections.label: Section 876
sections.original: ἄκλαυτος , ἄφιλος , ἀνυμέναιος ταλαίφρων ἄγομαι
sections.translation: Unwept , unfriended , without marriage-song , I am led in misery on this journey that cannot be put off . No longer is it permitted me , unhappy girl ,

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: θέμις ὁρᾶν ταλαίνᾳ .
sections.translation: to look up at this sacred eye of the burning sun . But for my fate no tear is shed , no friend moans in sorrow .

sections.label: Section 883
sections.original: ἆρʼ ἴστʼ , ἀοιδὰς καὶ γόους πρὸ τοῦ θανεῖν
sections.translation: Do you not know that dirges and wailing before death would never be given up , if it were allowed to make them freely ?

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: οὐκ ἄξεθʼ ὡς τάχιστα ; καὶ κατηρεφεῖ
sections.translation: Take her away — now ! And when you have enshrouded her , as I proclaimed , in her covered tomb , leave her alone , deserted — let her decide whether she wishes to die or to live entombed in such a home . It makes no difference , since our hands are clean so far as regards this girl .

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: μετοικίας δʼ οὖν τῆς ἄνω στερήσεται .
sections.translation: But no matter what , she will be stripped of her home here above .

sections.label: Section 891
sections.original: ὦ τύμβος , ὦ νυμφεῖον , ὦ κατασκαφὴς
sections.translation: Tomb , bridal-chamber , deep-dug eternal prison where I go to find my own , whom in the greatest numbers destruction has seized and Persephone has welcomed among the dead !

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: ὧν λοισθία ʼγὼ καὶ κάκιστα δὴ μακρῷ
sections.translation: Last of them all and in by far the most shameful circumstances , I will descend , even before the fated term of my life is spent . But I cherish strong hopes that I will arrive welcome to my father , and pleasant to you , Mother , and welcome , dear brother , to you .

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: ἐπεὶ θανόντας αὐτόχειρ ὑμᾶς ἐγὼ
sections.translation: For , when each of you died , with my own hands I washed and dressed you and poured drink-offerings at your graves . But now , Polyneices , it is for tending your corpse that I win such reward as this . And yet I honored you rightly , as the wise understand .

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: οὐ γάρ ποτʼ οὔτʼ ἄν , εἰ τέκνων μήτηρ ἔφυν ,
sections.translation: Never , if I had been a mother of children , or if a husband had been rotting after death , would I have taken that burden upon myself in violation of the citizens ’ will . For the sake of what law , you ask , do I say that ? A husband lost , another might have been found ,

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: καὶ παῖς ἀπʼ ἄλλου φωτός , εἰ τοῦδʼ ἤμπλακον ,
sections.translation: and if bereft of a child , there could be a second from some other man . But when father and mother are hidden in Hades , no brother could ever bloom for me again . Such was the law whereby I held you first in honor , but for that Creon judged me guilty of wrongdoing

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: καὶ δεινὰ τολμᾶν , ὦ κασίγνητον κάρα .
sections.translation: and of dreadful outrage , dear brother ! And now he leads me thus in his hands ’ strong grasp , when I have enjoyed no marriage bed or bridal song and have not received any portion of marriage or the nurture of children . But deserted by friends ,

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: ζῶσʼ εἰς θανόντων ἔρχομαι κατασκαφάς .
sections.translation: in misery I go living to the hollow graves of the dead . What law of the gods have I transgressed ? Why should I look to the gods anymore ? What ally should I call out to , when by my reverence I have earned a name for irreverence ?

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ μὲν οὖν τάδʼ ἐστὶν ἐν θεοῖς καλά ,
sections.translation: Well , then , if these events please the gods , once I have suffered my doom I will come to know my guilt . But if the guilt lies with my judges , I could wish for them no greater evils than they inflict unjustly on me .

sections.label: Section 929
sections.original: ἔτι τῶν αὐτῶν ἀνέμων αὑταὶ
sections.translation: Still the same tempest of the soul

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: ψυχῆς ῥιπαὶ τήνδε γʼ ἔχουσιν .
sections.translation: grips this girl with the same fierce gusts .

sections.label: Section 931
sections.original: τοιγὰρ τούτων τοῖσιν ἄγουσιν
sections.translation: Then because of this her guards will have reason to lament their slowness .
